title: entitlements — trait composition
description: Two owned traits: `HasEntitlements` mixed into Tenant (adds ->entitlements() relation + resolveEntitlement($key) accessor); `MetersUsage` mixed into consumer models (adds ->consumesEntitlement($key, $amount) helper). Owned entities also compose the standard foundation + tenants trait stack.

owned.description: Mixed into `Tenant`. Adds an `entitlements()` hasMany relation, a memoised `resolveEntitlement(string $key): ?Entitlement` accessor for fast in-request lookups, and a `getResolvedEntitlementsMap(): array` for /auth/me hydration.

Helper trait for domain models that consume metered features (WebhookDelivery, NotificationDelivery, NewsletterCampaign counters, etc.). Adds `consumesEntitlement(string $key, int $amount = 1, ?string $correlationId = null): void` which delegates to Enforcer::consume(). No stored state.

framework.reason: Only Entitlement uses soft delete. EntitlementUsage is high-volume + append-only — hard purge on retention.

foundation.reason: Track who applied a platform override on Entitlement.

tenants.reason: Every Entitlement + EntitlementUsage row scoped to a tenant.

activity.reason: Entitlement overrides + resets surface in the tenant activity feed.

audit.reason: Entitlement override history is compliance-material (contract obligations). Applied to Entitlement, NOT to EntitlementUsage (volume too high).

entitlement.notes: HasAudit critical: platform-admin overrides on enterprise contracts are auditable events.

entitlement_usage.notes: NO HasUserstamps (too high-volume to userstamp every consume), NO HasAudit (aggregate is what matters, not per-row), NO SoftDeletes (append-only + hard-purge on retention).
